tickets.description: Revise the Core-managed monitor plan so monitor execution is handled by a separate Core worker app/process instead of the Core API main instance, including ownership, deployment, scheduling, locking, and task breakdown implications.
acceptance: The plan document recommends a separate Core monitor worker, explains API versus worker responsibilities, includes deployment and concurrency implications, and Maat has a review note for the decision.

tickets.title: Convert Core monitor milestones into Maat rows
tickets.description: Convert the Core-managed monitor plan milestones into row-based milestone and ticket tables, then load the approved milestone structure into Maat as milestone goals with implementation tickets under each goal.
acceptance: The plan document shows milestone rows with ticket rows under each milestone; matching Maat goals and tickets are created with outcomes, descriptions, and acceptance criteria; the conversion ticket is completed with doc commit and Maat evidence.

tickets.title: Convert alert rollout phases into Maat goals
tickets.description: Convert the alert routing redesign rollout phases into concrete Maat goals and implementation tickets, then remove the temporary planning document from the repository.
acceptance: Maat contains one alert rollout goal per phase with at least one acceptance-backed ticket under each, the conversion ticket records evidence, and docs/plans/alert-routing-redesign.md is removed from the repo in a commit.

tickets.title: Expose current alert destinations and tests
tickets.description: Update the existing alert product surface so Console no longer behaves as webhook-only: show email channels if present, use destination language where possible, add webhook/email test notification actions, expand delivery log filters, and make current global alert rules understandable.
acceptance: Console and Core expose existing webhook and email alert behavior honestly, test notification actions work for supported channel types, delivery logs can be filtered by type/channel/event/incident, and the rules view explains global cooldown and recovery behavior.

tickets.title: Design Core monitor ownership and migration
tickets.description: Choose and document the database ownership model for Core-managed monitors, including Core owner rows or owner_kind fields, incident compatibility, monitor response fields, and migration order.
acceptance: Ownership design is recorded, migration steps are clear, existing Agent monitors keep working, and required Core/API/Console response changes are identified.

tickets.title: Model SMTP services and email destinations
tickets.description: Add reusable SMTP service records and email destinations that reference them, migrate secret storage away from duplicated per-channel fields, provide connection and send-test flows, and keep legacy email channels readable during migration.
acceptance: Core persists SMTP services and email destinations with redacted API responses, Console can create/test them, existing email channels remain readable, and alert delivery can send through the new SMTP service model.

tickets.title: Convert status page plan into Maat rollout
tickets.description: Translate docs/architecture/status-pages.md implementation phases and open decisions into concrete Maat goals and tickets without duplicating existing component mapping work
acceptance: Maat contains actionable status-page goals and tickets for manual pages, incident integration, uptime/history, subscriptions, customization, and architectural decisions

tickets.title: Implement explicit alert routes and dry runs
tickets.description: Create alert routes that replace implicit channel event subscriptions over time, with filters for event type, severity, monitor owner, and maintenance behavior, target destinations, and a dry-run endpoint for explaining route decisions.
acceptance: Alert routes can be created, read, updated, and evaluated; route dry-run returns match, suppression, grouping, and destination decisions; existing channel subscriptions are represented by a generated default route during migration.

tickets.title: Build Core monitor worker app foundation
tickets.description: Add the initial Core monitor worker binary under the agreed app path, sharing Core config, logging, database setup, migrations, and service construction without running API routes.
acceptance: The worker can start, connect to the Core database or internal API path, expose basic health logging, shut down gracefully, and run without blocking or embedding the Core API server.

tickets.title: Implement alert grouping policies and summaries
tickets.description: Add grouping policies for related alert events by incident, monitor, service label, owner, or severity, including delayed summary notifications and visible grouped or suppressed delivery decisions.
acceptance: Routes can use grouping policies, Core stores alert groups and grouped decisions, delayed summaries are delivered, and the delivery log shows grouped or suppressed events with reasons.

tickets.title: Add due-check leasing and scheduling
tickets.description: Implement the worker scheduling loop and due-check claim mechanism for Core monitor configs using leases or an equivalent durable coordination model.
acceptance: Due Core monitor configs can be claimed once, overdue checks recover after restart, leases expire safely, paused monitors are skipped, and tests cover duplicate-claim prevention.

tickets.title: Add alert delivery queue and retry attempts
tickets.description: Split alert deliveries from transport attempts, add retry scheduling with backoff, run delivery through a worker, and record SMTP/webhook failure stages for operational debugging.
acceptance: Core stores delivery attempts separately from deliveries, retries transient failures with bounded backoff, exposes attempt history in the API, and delivery logs show transport-specific failure stage and retry status.

tickets.title: Add HTTP status monitor runner
tickets.description: Implement the first Core worker check runner for HTTP and HTTPS status checks, including timeout handling, redirect policy, status matching, latency metrics, and structured report payloads.
acceptance: A Core-owned HTTP status monitor can be executed by the worker, report up/down results with latency and error context, and pass focused runner tests.

tickets.title: Add rich alert payloads and integrations
tickets.description: Add versioned webhook payloads, richer email templates, signing helpers, and additional destination integrations such as Slack or Discord after the routing and grouping foundation is stable.
acceptance: Alert destinations can choose a stable payload/template version, webhook signing can be configured and verified, HTML email templates are available, and at least one additional integration is planned or implemented behind the destination model.

tickets.title: Wire Core monitor reports into incident reconciliation
tickets.description: Route worker-produced Core monitor reports through the existing monitor health and incident reconciliation path without regressing Agent monitor report behavior.
acceptance: Worker reports update monitor health, create and resolve incidents, preserve Agent report behavior, and are covered by integration tests for failing and recovering Core monitors.

tickets.title: Add Core worker diagnostics and deployment wiring
tickets.description: Add deployment and diagnostics support for running the Core API and Core monitor worker as separate processes in local development and Docker Compose.
acceptance: Deployment docs/config start the API and worker separately, diagnostics expose worker state, and Core API health remains distinct from worker monitor execution health.

tickets.title: Add status page publication schema
tickets.description: Add Core migrations and models for status_pages, status_page_sections, status_page_components, component mappings, public incidents, and public incident updates as a publication layer over existing Core data
acceptance: Core has migrated tables and model/service tests proving pages, sections, components, mappings, incidents, and updates can be created without changing Agent reporting behavior

tickets.title: Add Core monitor create/edit/test API
tickets.description: Add admin API routes for creating, editing, pausing, resuming, deleting, testing, and retrieving redacted configs for Core-managed monitors.
acceptance: Routes validate monitor config, return redacted data, support test-now behavior, regenerate OpenAPI and SDK when needed, and include API tests for validation and lifecycle actions.

tickets.title: Build status page admin API
tickets.description: Add JWT-protected Core routes for listing, creating, editing, previewing, publishing, and unpublishing status pages, sections, components, mappings, and manual public incidents
acceptance: Admin routes are documented in OpenAPI, covered by Core tests, and regenerate the Console SDK without hand-editing generated files

tickets.title: Build Console Core monitor create workflow
tickets.description: Build the Console workflow for choosing a Core monitor type, entering target settings, configuring success criteria, testing the monitor, and saving it.
acceptance: A user can create a Core monitor from Console, test failures show actionable errors, saved monitors appear immediately, and the Console build passes.

tickets.title: Build Console status page editor
tickets.description: Add Console screens for status page list, draft editor, sections, public components, internal resource mapping, preview, and publish controls
acceptance: An admin can create a draft page, configure sections and components, map monitors or agents, preview the public DTO, and publish from the Console with URL-backed state where appropriate

tickets.title: Serve public status page payloads
tickets.description: Add unauthenticated public read routes for published status pages that return a dedicated sanitized public DTO instead of internal Console API structs
acceptance: GET /status/:slug returns only published page, component, public incident, and maintenance fields with no internal ids, raw payloads, secrets, or private incident notes

tickets.title: Add status page publish validation
tickets.description: Block or warn before publishing unsafe or incomplete status pages, including empty pages, unmapped components without manual status, conflicting slugs, and private-looking labels
acceptance: Publish attempts return actionable validation results and tests cover missing mappings, duplicate slugs, no visible components, and labels that look like internal hosts or IPs

tickets.title: Add Console owner and monitor type filters
tickets.description: Update Console monitor list filtering so users can filter by owner kind, owner name, monitor type, and Core versus Agent monitor source.
acceptance: Monitor lists expose stable owner/type filters, URL state stays consistent with existing table patterns, and Core/Agent ownership is visually clear in rows.

tickets.title: Add Core monitor detail config summary
tickets.description: Update monitor detail views to show Core monitor ownership, schedule, next run, last run, target summary, redacted config, latest report, and worker execution context.
acceptance: Core monitor detail pages explain what is checked, who checks it, when it runs next, what the latest result was, and never expose secret values.

tickets.title: Link internal incidents to public incidents
tickets.description: Allow an admin to create a draft public status page incident linked to an existing internal Orion incident while keeping public title, status, severity, impact, and updates separate from internal incident events
acceptance: A linked public incident can be drafted, published, and resolved without exposing internal incident notes, ids, alert delivery state, or raw monitor details

tickets.title: Redact Core monitor secrets in Console and API responses
tickets.description: Ensure Core monitor secrets such as headers, tokens, credentials, and browser-flow secret values are write-only or redacted in API responses, logs, reports, and Console views.
acceptance: Secret-bearing fields are redacted on read, raw reports and logs do not leak secrets, update flows preserve or replace secrets intentionally, and tests cover redaction.

tickets.title: Suggest affected status page components
tickets.description: Use status page component mappings to suggest which public components are affected when an internal incident is opened or updated
acceptance: Console can show suggested affected public components for an internal incident, and Core tests cover monitor-to-component and agent-to-component mapping suggestions

tickets.title: Add heartbeat token and ingest routes
tickets.description: Add generated heartbeat monitor tokens and public ingest routes for success and failure signals from cron jobs, backups, scripts, and scheduled tasks.
acceptance: Heartbeat success and failure routes validate tokens, store report payloads with truncation, update last signal timestamps, and reject invalid or deleted monitor tokens.

tickets.title: Implement public incident update workflow
tickets.description: Add API and Console flows for drafting, publishing, editing, and resolving public status page incident updates with public statuses investigating, identified, monitoring, resolved, and scheduled
acceptance: Admins can publish public incident timeline updates and resolution messages, while public pages show only published updates in chronological order

tickets.title: Record status page publish audit events
tickets.description: Record auditable events for status page publish, unpublish, public incident publish, public incident update, resolution, and component mapping changes
acceptance: Audit records identify the action, status page, affected object, timestamp, and actor without storing secrets or raw incident payloads

tickets.title: Add heartbeat missed-check reconciliation worker
tickets.description: Add worker-side reconciliation that marks heartbeat monitors down or stale when expected signals are missed beyond their configured interval and grace period.
acceptance: Missed heartbeat checks open incidents after the grace period, recovery signals resolve incidents through the shared reconciliation path, and tests cover pending, missed, failed, and recovered states.

tickets.title: Add heartbeat Console copy and setup affordances
tickets.description: Add Console UI for heartbeat setup, including copyable success and failure endpoints, example curl snippets, last signal state, and setup guidance without turning the page into marketing copy.
acceptance: Users can copy heartbeat endpoints, see pending and last-signal state, inspect setup examples, and complete the workflow without editing backend files.

tickets.title: Aggregate public component uptime
tickets.description: Compute public component uptime from mapped monitor uptime rollups and recent raw monitor report data using each component's configured uptime rollup strategy
acceptance: Public component uptime returns daily buckets and overall uptime for mapped monitors, handles multiple mappings deterministically, and hides raw report counts by default

tickets.title: Add heartbeat failure payload inspection
tickets.description: Expose heartbeat failure payloads and recent heartbeat events in monitor detail views and incident timelines while enforcing truncation and redaction limits.
acceptance: Heartbeat details show recent success/failure payload context, incident timelines include heartbeat evidence, payloads are bounded, and secrets remain redacted.

tickets.title: Add public status history routes
tickets.description: Add public read routes for status page history, public incident history, and individual public incident detail using sanitized status page DTOs
acceptance: GET /status/:slug/history, GET /status/:slug/incidents, and GET /status/:slug/incidents/:incident_id return only published public data with bounded date ranges and tests for hidden draft incidents

tickets.title: Add monitor confirmation periods
tickets.description: Add per-monitor confirmation periods so a Core monitor must fail for enough time or enough checks before opening an incident.
acceptance: Transient failures within the confirmation window do not open incidents, sustained failures do open incidents, and reconciliation tests cover time-based and count-based behavior.

tickets.title: Cache public status page read models
tickets.description: Add short-lived caching, invalidation, and optional ETag support for public status page payloads so read-heavy public pages stay cheap while health and published updates remain fresh
acceptance: Public page responses use safe cache headers, invalidate after publish or health-relevant changes, and tests cover stale cache prevention for newly published incident updates

tickets.title: Decide public uptime formatting
tickets.description: Choose how public uptime percentages and unknown states should be represented, including decimal precision, unknown handling, and whether timestamps are exact or rounded
acceptance: A documented decision defines uptime percentage formatting, unknown state display behavior, timestamp rounding rules, and the UI/API implications

tickets.title: Add monitor recovery periods
tickets.description: Add per-monitor recovery periods so incidents resolve only after a monitor has remained healthy long enough to avoid recovery flapping.
acceptance: Short healthy blips do not resolve incidents, sustained healthy checks resolve incidents, and tests cover open, recovering, resolved, and relapse paths.

tickets.title: Add Core monitor flapping handling
tickets.description: Detect and represent flapping Core monitor behavior so repeated up/down transitions become visible as degraded or flapping context instead of noisy incident churn.
acceptance: Flapping is detected from recent report history, reflected in computed health or incident context, and covered by tests for repeated transitions.

tickets.title: Add Core monitor severity defaults
tickets.description: Define and implement default incident severity behavior for Core monitor types, with room for per-monitor overrides where useful.
acceptance: Core monitor incidents receive predictable severity defaults by type or config, overrides are validated, and incident list/detail views show the selected severity.

tickets.title: Design public subscriber model
tickets.description: Define the status_page_subscriptions data model, confirmation state, destination types, component-scoped subscriptions, unsubscribe behavior, and privacy boundaries
acceptance: A design or schema change defines subscriber fields, confirmation lifecycle, component scoping, unsubscribe flow, and how public subscribers stay separate from internal alert channels

tickets.title: Add Core monitor maintenance windows
tickets.description: Add maintenance-window behavior for Core-managed monitors so scheduled work or intentional outages suppress incidents and alert delivery while preserving history.
acceptance: Core monitor maintenance windows suppress incident creation and alert delivery, are visible in Console, and tests cover entering, active, and exiting maintenance windows.

tickets.title: Implement subscription confirmation flow
tickets.description: Add public subscription creation, confirmation, and unsubscribe endpoints with rate limiting and safe token handling
acceptance: Visitors can request, confirm, and cancel subscriptions; endpoints are rate limited; tokens are not logged or exposed; tests cover invalid and expired confirmation attempts

tickets.title: Design component data model
tickets.description: Design the service/status-page component model that can group Agent and Core monitors and support incident impact and status page publishing.
acceptance: Design records component fields, relationships, migration path, API shape, Console needs, and how Agent/Core monitor ownership maps to components.

tickets.title: Fan out public incident notifications
tickets.description: Deliver published public incident updates to confirmed status page subscribers without sending internal incident details or alert delivery metadata
acceptance: Confirmed subscribers receive public incident updates for subscribed pages or components, delivery failures are tracked safely, and internal alert channel secrets are never exposed

tickets.title: Add public status feed
tickets.description: Add an Atom or RSS feed for published status page incidents and updates so visitors can follow status changes without an account
acceptance: GET /status/:slug/feed.atom or the chosen feed route returns valid feed output for published public incidents only, with tests for draft and private incidents being omitted

tickets.title: Add monitor-to-component mapping
tickets.description: Implement mapping between monitors and service/status-page components so Core and Agent monitor health can roll up into component health.
acceptance: Monitors can be assigned to components, component health can be derived from mapped monitors, and Console/API paths expose the mapping safely.

tickets.title: Add incident component fields
tickets.description: Extend incidents and incident timeline/context so monitor-caused incidents can identify impacted service/status-page components.
acceptance: Incident records or responses include impacted component context, existing incidents remain compatible, and incident list/detail views can display component impact.

tickets.title: Add status page theme settings
tickets.description: Add configurable public status page theme settings such as logo, accent color, page description, and basic metadata while preserving accessible default styling
acceptance: Admins can configure supported theme fields, public pages render those fields accessibly, and invalid or unsafe theme values are rejected

tickets.title: Update status page architecture for monitor components
tickets.description: Update status page planning so public or private status pages consume component health derived from Core and Agent monitors.
acceptance: Status page architecture documents monitor-component health flow, component display rules, maintenance interaction, and implementation dependencies.

tickets.title: Support status page custom domains
tickets.description: Design and implement custom domain configuration for public status pages, including validation, ownership guidance, routing, and safe host matching
acceptance: Admins can configure a custom domain for a status page, Core routes requests to the correct published page, and invalid or conflicting domains are rejected with clear errors

tickets.title: Add public embeddable status badges
tickets.description: Add public embeddable badge endpoints or snippets that expose a status page or component's current public state without leaking internal resource details
acceptance: Badge output renders the selected public page or component status, includes cache headers, and omits internal ids, monitor names, and private incident data

tickets.title: Add HTTP keyword monitor
tickets.description: Implement Core worker HTTP keyword checks for required and forbidden response body content, building on the HTTP status runner.
acceptance: Keyword monitors can assert required and forbidden content, report match failure context with bounded body capture, and pass runner tests.

tickets.title: Add public status page metadata
tickets.description: Add optional SEO and Open Graph metadata for published status pages so shared links describe the public page and current status clearly
acceptance: Published status pages expose safe title, description, canonical URL, and Open Graph metadata with no internal resource names unless explicitly configured

tickets.title: Add expected status code monitor
tickets.description: Implement a Core worker HTTP expected-status monitor path for APIs and endpoints that should return specific status codes or code sets.
acceptance: Expected-status monitors validate configured status codes, store status and latency metrics, show clear failure messages, and reuse safe HTTP runner behavior.

tickets.title: Choose public status page serving model
tickets.description: Decide whether public status pages are served from Core's main binary, a separate public frontend bundle, or another deployment shape
acceptance: A documented decision records the chosen serving model, routing implications, asset build path, authentication boundary, and deployment impact

tickets.title: Add TCP port monitor
tickets.description: Implement Core worker TCP port checks that attempt a bounded connection to a host and port and record connection timing and failure details.
acceptance: TCP monitors handle success, refused connections, DNS failures, and timeouts with structured report payloads and runner tests.

tickets.title: Decide single versus multiple status pages
tickets.description: Decide whether the first status page release supports multiple pages or starts with one default page and later expands
acceptance: A documented decision defines first-release page cardinality, slug behavior, Console navigation, API shape, and migration path if multiple pages are deferred

tickets.title: Add TLS certificate monitor
tickets.description: Implement Core worker TLS certificate checks that validate certificate chain behavior and expiration thresholds for HTTPS hosts.
acceptance: TLS monitors report days remaining, issuer/subject summary, threshold failures, connection errors, and tests for healthy, expiring, and invalid certificates.

tickets.title: Decide public incident automation policy
tickets.description: Decide when internal incident changes should automatically create draft public incidents, suggest resolution updates, or require manual administrator action
acceptance: A documented decision defines default incident publication policy, auto-draft behavior, auto-resolution suggestions, and any trusted-component automation rules

tickets.title: Add DNS monitor
tickets.description: Implement Core worker DNS record checks for configured hostnames, record types, and expected values.
acceptance: DNS monitors support A, AAAA, CNAME, TXT, MX, and NS checks, report resolver and answer context, handle lookup failures, and pass runner tests.

tickets.title: Decide subscription infrastructure reuse
tickets.description: Decide whether public status page subscriptions reuse internal alert channel infrastructure or use a separate subscriber and delivery system
acceptance: A documented decision compares reuse versus separation for privacy, delivery reliability, unsubscribe handling, rate limits, and future channel support

tickets.title: Add ping monitor
tickets.description: Implement Core worker ping-style reachability checks with clear behavior for environments where ICMP is unavailable or not permitted.
acceptance: Ping monitors record reachability and latency when supported, expose fallback or unsupported-state behavior when ICMP is unavailable, and tests cover success, timeout, and permission cases.

tickets.title: Add domain expiration monitor
tickets.description: Implement Core worker domain expiration checks using RDAP first and a documented fallback strategy for registries that do not expose usable data.
acceptance: Domain monitors report expiration date and days remaining when available, fail clearly when data cannot be determined, and tests cover threshold and unavailable-data behavior.

tickets.title: Add API request monitor
tickets.description: Implement Core worker API request checks with method, headers, body, auth secret references, expected status, and JSON response assertions.
acceptance: API monitors can run configured requests, redact secrets, evaluate status and JSON assertions, store bounded response context, and pass tests for success, assertion failure, and transport failure.

tickets.title: Add UDP monitor
tickets.description: Implement Core worker UDP checks with explicit payload and expected response semantics so UDP success is service-specific and not ambiguous.
acceptance: UDP monitors send configured payloads, validate expected responses, report timeout or mismatch details, and document unsupported no-response semantics.

tickets.title: Implement incident lifecycle action workflow
tickets.description: Add Core and Console support for incident acknowledge, manual resolve, mark covered, and reopen actions, including service methods, API routes, OpenAPI annotations, generated SDK updates, action dialogs, and query invalidation.
acceptance: Incident detail exposes working lifecycle actions; Core persists lifecycle fields and incident events; OpenAPI and Console SDK are regenerated; tests cover allowed transitions, monitor active incident updates, and notification behavior.

tickets.title: Add SMTP IMAP and POP monitors
tickets.description: Implement Core worker mail protocol availability checks for SMTP, IMAP, and POP with TLS mode, banner or capability checks, and optional credential support later.
acceptance: Mail monitors can check configured protocol endpoints, report banner/capability and TLS context, handle auth-disabled mode safely, and pass protocol runner tests.

tickets.title: Add Playwright transaction monitor
tickets.description: Implement Core worker browser transaction checks using Playwright with sandboxing, timeout budgets, secret handling, screenshots on failure, and artifact retention limits.
acceptance: Playwright monitors can run bounded transactions, capture failure artifacts, redact secrets, avoid overloading the worker, and pass tests or smoke checks for success and failure flows.

tickets.title: Add covered incident suppression semantics
tickets.description: Teach incident reconciliation to honor active coverage after a user marks an incident covered, suppress duplicate incident creation until recovery or expiry, and record coverage-related audit events.
acceptance: A covered still-failing monitor does not immediately open a duplicate incident; coverage ends on recovery or expiry; incident events record suppressed-by-coverage behavior; Console shows coverage state and offers an end coverage or reopen path.

tickets.title: Add synthetic multi-step monitor
tickets.description: Implement Core worker synthetic multi-step checks for ordered API or browser steps with shared variables, assertions, stop-on-failure behavior, and bounded artifacts.
acceptance: Synthetic monitors run ordered steps, preserve step-level results, fail with actionable context, respect timeout and artifact limits, and pass tests for success and step failure.

tickets.title: Expose incident evidence and raw report inspection
tickets.description: Extend incident detail responses and Console views with normalized triggering report, latest report, related incidents, safe raw payload inspection, and timeline links back to reports or deliveries.
acceptance: Incident detail shows trigger versus latest evidence; raw monitor report payloads are inspectable from incident and monitor history; timeline rows link to the relevant report or alert delivery; tests cover response shape and safe payload exposure.

tickets.title: Add incident analytics and lifecycle filters
tickets.description: Add Core filters and aggregate data for incident resolution kind, actor, covered state, recurring monitor failures, notification reliability, and mean time to acknowledge or resolve; surface the most useful insights in Console.
acceptance: Incidents can be filtered by resolution kind, actor, and covered state; Core exposes aggregate metrics for recurring failures and lifecycle timing; Console summary or detail views show actionable incident insights without replacing the operational tables.

tickets.title: Spawn Core monitor milestone agents
tickets.description: Spawn one supervising agent per Core monitor milestone goal. Each milestone agent should spawn one ticket worker per ticket under that milestone, collect worker reports, validate acceptance criteria, and report milestone status back.
acceptance: Six milestone supervisor agents are spawned with the correct goal IDs, ticket IDs, delegation instructions, validation duties, and reporting expectations.

tickets.title: Spawn incident phase goal agents
tickets.description: Start one supervising agent for each incident detail phase goal and instruct each supervising agent to spawn ticket-level workers, validate their outputs, and report completion evidence back through Maat.
acceptance: Four incident phase goal agents are spawned with the correct goal and ticket assignments, delegation instructions, validation expectations, and Maat reporting requirements.

tickets.title: Set up alert goal agent hierarchy
tickets.description: Spawn one supervising goal agent per alert rollout goal, instruct each goal agent to sponsor ticket agents for its tickets, validate ticket outputs, and report validation status back.
acceptance: Each alert rollout goal has a named supervising agent with ticket-agent delegation instructions recorded in Maat or reported in-thread, and the parent thread records any limits or validation gaps.

tickets.title: Coordinate status page subscription tickets
tickets.description: Coordinate ticket-level workers for the status page subscriptions goal, validate their evidence, and report ticket states without pushing remotes.
acceptance: Each listed ticket has a spawned worker report, validation notes are recorded, and Maat ticket completion only happens with concrete evidence.

tickets.title: Coordinate manual public status pages delivery
tickets.description: Coordinate ticket-level worker agents for manual public status pages, validate their output against docs/architecture/status-pages.md and Maat acceptance, and report ticket evidence without pushing remotes.
acceptance: All listed status page tickets have worker reports reviewed, validation notes recorded, and any safe completions or blockers reflected in Maat.

tickets.title: Expose alert delivery filters and test actions in Console
tickets.description: Update the Console alerts experience so notification logs expose the Core-supported type, channel, and event_type filters, and channel rows provide a manual test action for webhook and email alert destinations.
acceptance: Console users can filter alert deliveries by type, channel, and event type using URL-backed table state where appropriate, and can trigger a manual test for supported alert destinations with success and failure feedback.

tickets.title: Add SMTP service and email destination Console management
tickets.description: Add Console UI for listing, creating, editing, deleting, and safely displaying reusable SMTP services and email destinations backed by the Core alert SMTP APIs.
acceptance: Console users can manage SMTP services and email destinations without exposing secrets, see validation errors from Core, and the UI builds and lints successfully.

tickets.title: Add dedicated SMTP and email destination tests
tickets.description: Add Core endpoints and service behavior for testing SMTP services and email destinations directly, rather than relying only on the legacy alert channel test endpoint.
acceptance: Core exposes authenticated test endpoints for SMTP service connectivity and email destination send tests, OpenAPI is regenerated, and focused API/service tests cover success and failure responses without leaking secrets.

tickets.title: Complete alert route coverage and maintenance semantics
tickets.description: Close the remaining alert route validation gaps by adding API coverage for list/update/delete behavior and deciding whether maintenance-aware routing requires a first-class route field or an explicit documented non-goal.
acceptance: Alert route list, update, delete, and dry-run paths have focused tests; any maintenance routing behavior is either implemented in Core/OpenAPI or documented as intentionally out of scope for this phase with Maat evidence.

tickets.title: Complete grouping policies and summary delivery
tickets.description: Finish alert grouping policy support by adding selectable grouping policy fields on routes, delayed grouped summary delivery, and delivery API exposure for alert_group_id.
acceptance: Routes can select grouping behavior, grouped incidents can produce delayed summaries instead of only immediate suppression, alert_group_id is exposed in delivery responses/OpenAPI/SDK, and focused Core tests cover the policy and summary behavior.

tickets.title: Apply signed webhooks and HTML email rendering
tickets.description: Promote the alert payload helpers into configurable delivery behavior by applying webhook signatures when configured and adding HTML email rendering alongside plain text.
acceptance: Webhook destinations can opt into signed deliveries with tested headers, email deliveries include tested HTML and plain-text bodies, payload/template version behavior remains stable, and Core/OpenAPI/docs are updated where contracts change.

tickets.title: Add Slack and Discord alert destinations
tickets.description: Add first-class Slack and Discord alert destination support or a documented integration adapter plan that fits the alert destination model and payload versioning.
acceptance: Slack and Discord destinations have a clear Core model and delivery path with tests, or a reviewed plan document records the adapter contract, payload shape, configuration fields, and phased implementation tickets.

tickets.title: Coordinate status page uptime history tickets
tickets.description: Coordinate the status page uptime and history ticket workers, validate their evidence before completion, and report blocked dependency edges or delegation constraints
acceptance: Each target ticket has a spawned or explicitly blocked worker status, validation notes are recorded, and no ticket is completed without evidence

tickets.title: Add Slack and Discord alert channel validation
tickets.description: Add first-class slack and discord alert channel types to Core alert channel create/update validation and API coverage without changing the generic signed webhook payload path.
acceptance: Core API tests show slack and discord channels require webhook_url, persist subscribed events, and appear in alert channel responses with delivery metadata.

tickets.title: Add chat alert renderers and delivery tests
tickets.description: Implement Slack and Discord chat webhook renderers from the settled AlertPayload contract and wire delivery dispatch after the signed-webhook and HTML email payload work lands.
acceptance: Core service tests prove manual tests and incident route delivery send provider-shaped Slack and Discord JSON, record delivery types, and retry through the existing alert delivery path.

tickets.title: Add Console chat alert destination controls
tickets.description: Add Console management affordances for Slack and Discord alert destinations once Core exposes the stable channel contract and generated SDK types.
acceptance: Console can create, update, list, and test Slack and Discord alert destinations through the generated SDK without hand-editing generated code.

tickets.title: Decide public subscriber mail sender config
tickets.description: Document which mail sender configuration should power public status page subscriber confirmation and fan-out emails without exposing internal alert channel secrets.
acceptance: A status page architecture decision records the public subscriber mail sender source, token URL origin behavior, sender identity, bounce/reply handling, rate-limit expectations, and separation from internal alert channels.

tickets.title: Integrate Core monitor worker foundations
tickets.description: Integrate the completed Core monitor worker foundation, scheduling, incident reconciliation, and diagnostics worktrees onto the current product branch, resolving migration numbering and generated contract conflicts without dropping status page work.
acceptance: The current branch contains the Core monitor worker app, due-check leasing schema/service, report-to-incident reconciliation, and worker diagnostics routes with migrations renumbered after existing status page migrations; Core tests and relevant generation commands pass or blockers are recorded with exact evidence.

tickets.title: Coordinate M1 HTTP runner closeout
tickets.description: Coordinate the remaining M1 Core monitor HTTP runner work by spawning a goal agent, validating its ticket-worker output, and integrating only verified code onto the current product branch.
acceptance: A goal agent has reported on the HTTP status monitor runner ticket, validated changes are integrated or blockers are recorded with evidence, and Maat has completion or progress notes for the coordination work.

tickets.title: Coordinate M6 monitor catalog expansion
tickets.description: Coordinate the full monitor catalog expansion now that the Core worker HTTP runner foundation is landed, spawning ticket workers in conflict-aware waves and validating their output before integration.
acceptance: M6 ticket workers are spawned or queued in disjoint implementation waves, at least one monitor catalog ticket has validated output or a concrete blocker, and Maat records progress with evidence.

tickets.title: Coordinate M6 network monitor wave
tickets.description: Coordinate the next M6 catalog wave for network-oriented Core worker monitors, starting with TCP and using goal/ticket agents for DNS and TLS exploration while parent integrates a validated slice.
acceptance: At least one network monitor ticket is implemented and validated or blocked with evidence, DNS/TLS goal-agent status is checked, and Maat records progress for the wave.

tickets.title: Split Core worker monitor runners
tickets.description: Refactor the Core worker runner implementations out of the worker app loop into focused runner files so DNS, TLS, and later catalog types can be added without repeatedly editing one oversized app file.
acceptance: HTTP and TCP runner behavior is unchanged, runner code is split into focused files with narrow imports, and apps/core worker/Core tests still pass.

tickets.title: Implement dedicated public subscriber mail sender
tickets.description: Add the configured public subscriber mail sender and delivery worker/adapter needed to send confirmation and public incident update emails from status page subscriber delivery rows without reading internal alert routes or exposing alert channel secrets.
acceptance: A configured public sender can deliver confirmation and public incident update emails to confirmed subscribers; missing or invalid sender configuration records safe delivery failures; token URLs are built from configured public origin or validated custom domain; tests prove no internal alert destinations, routes, SMTP secrets, raw subscriber tokens, or internal incident fields are exposed.

tickets.title: Capture Console first-run verification
tickets.description: Open the deployed Orion Console for the first-run Core, sign in with the configured admin credentials, and verify the Agents and monitor views show exactly the registered first-run Agent and configured monitor rows after their first interval.
acceptance: Evidence includes the Console URL, sign-in success, Agents view showing one Agent, monitor rows visible after the interval, and matching Agent/monitor IDs or names to the deployment checklist run.

tickets.title: Verify installed Agent service on first-run host
tickets.description: Run the deployment checklist against an installed Orion Agent service on a real first-run host. Confirm service manager state, installed config, installed state database, installed log file, restart behavior through the service manager, and no duplicate Agent or monitor rows after restart. Do not reuse the temporary native run-once smoke as the only evidence.
acceptance: Evidence records the installed service status, platform state and log paths, restart command/output, stable Agent ID, stable monitor IDs, and post-restart API or Console counts showing one Agent and the expected monitor count.

tickets.title: Expose scheduled maintenance windows on public status pages
tickets.description: Expose scheduled public incident start and end timestamps through the public status page DTOs and Console incident form so scheduled maintenance windows are visible on published status pages.
acceptance: Public status page incident payloads include scheduled_start_at and scheduled_end_at when present, OpenAPI and SDK are regenerated, Console status page incident forms can set scheduled start and end times, and tests cover public projection of scheduled maintenance windows.

tickets.title: Audit remaining Orion goal completion state
tickets.description: Audit active-looking Orion goals against Maat completion events and current repo evidence, using goal agents to identify which goals are truly done versus which need implementation, then record the next concrete work item.
acceptance: Maat records goal-agent audit results for the next unfinished goal clusters, at least one real remaining implementation ticket is identified or completed, and stale active-status bookkeeping is distinguished from product gaps.

tickets.title: Audit remaining incident and component goals
tickets.description: Audit the active-looking incident detail phase goals and M5 component/status-page groundwork against current Maat completion events and repo evidence, then identify the next real unfinished product ticket if any.
acceptance: Maat records goal-agent audit results for incident detail phases and M5 component groundwork, distinguishes stale active status from real product gaps, and names or completes the next concrete implementation ticket.

tickets.title: Implement Core monitor target allow and deny policy
tickets.description: Use docs/agent-core-contract.md and docs/plans/core-managed-monitors.md security notes to define and enforce Core monitor URL target policy, including link-local metadata protection, optional private-network allowance, redirect checks, and final host recording.
acceptance: Core monitor create, update, test, and worker execution reject blocked targets and redirects to blocked hosts by default, allow private targets only through an explicit setting or documented policy, record final URL/host safely, redact sensitive target details from public responses, and include Core tests for blocked link-local, blocked redirect, allowed public, and configured private-network cases.

tickets.title: Add type-specific Core monitor API validation
tickets.description: Replace generic Core monitor config acceptance for catalog monitor types with focused request validation for required fields, value ranges, safe defaults, and redaction behavior, using docs/milestones/m6-core-monitor-catalog.md and docs/plans/core-managed-monitors.md as source context.
acceptance: Core monitor create, update, and test APIs validate each supported monitor type's required fields and constraints, return clear 400 errors for invalid configs, preserve redaction of secrets and targets, regenerate OpenAPI/SDK when contracts change, and include focused Core API tests for representative valid and invalid configs.

tickets.title: Decide Playwright runtime packaging
tickets.description: Resolve and document how Playwright transaction monitors get their browser runtime on worker hosts, including whether Orion ships an optional image, sidecar, or explicit install path, then wire deployment docs accordingly.
acceptance: A decision document or deployment doc explains the supported Playwright runtime packaging model, Core worker startup and error messages point operators to that model, Docker/deployment assets are updated if an optional image or sidecar is chosen, and tests or smoke notes prove missing-runtime behavior remains explicit and non-silent.

tickets.title: Add WHOIS fallback for domain expiration monitors
tickets.description: Implement the docs/milestones/m6-core-monitor-catalog.md deferred fallback for domain expiration checks so RDAP remains first but unavailable RDAP data can fall back to a safe WHOIS strategy where possible.
acceptance: Domain expiration checks try RDAP first, fall back to WHOIS only for supported cases, report unavailable data clearly when both sources fail, preserve timeout and privacy safeguards, and include worker tests for RDAP success, RDAP unavailable plus WHOIS success, and both unavailable.

tickets.title: Define subscriber deletion and retention workflow
tickets.description: Turn docs/architecture/status-pages.md subscriber retention notes into an implemented or explicitly documented privacy workflow for hard deletion, retention policy, and audit-safe handling of unsubscribed public subscribers.
acceptance: There is a documented retention/deletion policy for public subscribers, any implemented hard-delete or anonymize action keeps unsubscribe/re-subscribe behavior deterministic, public routes do not reveal raw subscriber destinations, admin actions are audited where sensitive, and Core tests cover deletion/anonymization behavior if code is added.

tickets.title: Design Agent token rotation and revocation
tickets.description: Resolve the open docs/agent-core-contract.md token lifecycle decision by designing Core and Agent flows for rotating or revoking Agent tokens, including operational recovery and monitor continuity.
acceptance: A contract or architecture doc defines token rotation and revocation semantics, Core exposes or plans authenticated actions for rotation/revocation, Agent behavior on revoked credentials is explicit, existing monitor IDs are preserved where intended, and follow-up implementation tickets are created if the design is not implemented in the same change.

tickets.title: Implement Core Agent token lifecycle API
tickets.description: Implement the Core side of Agent token lifecycle controls from docs/architecture/agent-token-lifecycle.md: token hash/version/revocation metadata, admin-authenticated rotate revoke reissue and status actions, revoked-token auth rejection, registration safeguards for revoked machine_id recovery, one-time token responses, redaction, audit events, and preserved Agent/monitor identity.
acceptance: Core migrations, services, API handlers, route annotations, OpenAPI, and tests cover rotate revoke reissue and status; old tokens are rejected after rotation; revoked Agents cannot recover a token through unauthenticated /v1/register; agent_id machine_id monitor IDs reports incidents and maintenance state are preserved; normal Agent and frontend responses do not expose plaintext tokens.

tickets.title: Add Agent replacement token recovery command
tickets.description: Implement the Agent-side recovery UX from docs/architecture/agent-token-lifecycle.md: apply an admin-issued replacement token to existing local state without clearing agent_id or monitor mappings, keep reconfigure for intentional identity reset, and surface Core token revoked or invalid auth failures as redacted visible terminal errors.
acceptance: Agent CLI and state tests cover applying a replacement token while preserving agent_id core_url and monitor mappings; auth failures from protected routes stop reporting with a non-zero visible error and do not queue new reports with rejected credentials; docs explain when to use token apply versus reconfigure.

tickets.title: Add Console Agent token lifecycle controls
tickets.description: Add Console controls for the Agent token lifecycle design: show non-secret token status on Agent detail, provide admin rotate revoke and reissue actions, show one-time replacement tokens safely, and guide operators that rotation preserves identity while reconfigure can create a new identity.
acceptance: Console uses generated SDK types for token status and lifecycle actions; Agent detail shows active revoked rotated and version metadata without secrets; rotate and reissue display the new token once with no persistence; revoke requires confirmation and reason entry; build and browser smoke verify no token leaks in ordinary Agent views.

tickets.title: Document colleague feedback action plan
tickets.description: turn external feedback about examples, README clarity, architecture, deployment targets, terminology, repository hygiene, and planning visibility into a concrete action plan document in the Orion repo
acceptance: a committed docs action plan exists with prioritized tasks, owner areas, acceptance criteria, and follow-up decisions

tickets.title: Add status pages to demo seed data
tickets.description: Update the Core seed-demo-data script so seeded demo databases include representative status pages and related public status-page records.
acceptance: Running the seed-demo-data script creates status page rows with sections, components, mappings, public incidents/updates, subscribers or history where supported, and docs describe the seeded status-page data.

tickets.title: Rename product-facing Agent terminology to Server
tickets.description: replace product-facing Agent/Agents terminology with Server/Servers across Orion docs and Console copy while keeping the orion-agent CLI name and command surfaces intact
acceptance: README, architecture/deployment/planning docs, and Console labels use Server/Servers for monitored hosts; CLI references still use orion-agent where documenting commands or binary behavior; verification confirms remaining Agent references are intentional

tickets.title: Convert feedback plan into Maat goals
tickets.description: turn the colleague feedback action plan backlog into concrete Maat goals and starter tickets without duplicating already completed Server terminology work
acceptance: Maat contains separate goals and actionable tickets for README clarity, example app, quality badges, repository hygiene, Kubernetes posture, and planning hygiene follow-ups; completed terminology work is noted rather than duplicated

tickets.title: Update README why and architecture sections
tickets.description: expand the README for evaluators by adding a clear Why Orion section, component architecture, Core API boundary, alternative UI/headless posture, and Docker hosting guidance
acceptance: README explains why Orion over SaaS-style monitors, describes Server/Core/Console responsibilities, states whether alternative UIs can use Core APIs, documents Docker hosting expectations, and uses Server terminology throughout product-facing copy

tickets.title: Add Docker Compose Python example
tickets.description: add a minimal Python service that sleeps in a loop, wire it into a Docker Compose example, and document how to connect it to Orion
acceptance: repository contains a runnable example with compose files, the Python service starts successfully, docs show setup and verification steps, and the example demonstrates expected Orion Server/Core integration

tickets.title: Add CI and coverage badges
tickets.description: add visible README badges for CI and test coverage, or document the missing coverage publishing prerequisite before adding a badge
acceptance: README includes current CI status and a coverage badge when a coverage provider is configured; if coverage publishing is not configured, the ticket records the blocker and adds the smallest truthful quality signal available

tickets.title: Remove tracked DS Store files
tickets.description: remove committed .DS_Store files from the repository and make sure ignore rules prevent future macOS metadata commits
acceptance: git ls-files '*DS_Store' returns no tracked files, .gitignore covers .DS_Store, and the cleanup commit does not touch unrelated files

tickets.title: Write Kubernetes deployment decision note
tickets.description: document whether Orion should target Docker Compose, external Core hosting, Kubernetes clusters, and minikube examples for early users
acceptance: docs state the intended default deployment path, explain scenarios where Kubernetes is useful, list prerequisites for a minikube example, and call out secrets, ingress, persistence, and upgrade considerations

tickets.title: Audit public planning and AGENTS material
tickets.description: review AGENTS.md, docs/plans, and other public planning material for sensitive strategy, credentials, or private operational notes, then decide what stays public versus what belongs only in Maat
acceptance: audit records which planning files remain public, removes or creates follow-up tickets for sensitive material, and documents the boundary between repository plans and private Maat memory

tickets.title: Capture publishable plans follow-up for Maat
tickets.description: turn the need for publishable project plans into a tracked follow-up in the appropriate Maat project or document why Orion will handle public planning manually for now
acceptance: there is a linked Maat-project follow-up for publishable plans or an Orion planning note records the temporary manual workflow and privacy boundary

tickets.title: Open incident phase implementation PR
tickets.description: Integrate the validated incident phase worker commits into a clean PR branch toward main, run focused validation, push the branch, and open a GitHub pull request without disturbing unrelated local checkout changes.
acceptance: A pull request exists against main with the incident lifecycle, coverage, evidence, and analytics changes, including validation evidence and links to worker commits or integrated branch commits.

evidence: AGENTS.md was updated with the current Maat initialize command forms and committed in f5efed6 docs(repo): update maat instructions.
Delivered Markdown summary of Maat setup friction in chat and preserved the prior repo record at docs/development/maat-setup-friction.md from commit 247244f.

documents.title: Ticket: Verify v1 document-drive acceptance workflow
documents.content:
# Ticket: Verify v1 document-drive acceptance workflow

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215050-9b18 |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-214932-bfdb |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:50:50+02:00 |

## Description

Prove the product acceptance path from docs/product-plan.md using real or smoke-quality fixtures: drop or upload supported documents, process them, search source and main-language text, inspect document detail, organize with tags/collections, and retry failures without mutating originals.

## Acceptance

- A Dutch scanned PDF can be moved into the configured document directory and found by a Dutch query.
- The same document can be found through the selected main language after translation.
- A DOCX keeps its base format and can be searched, downloaded, and inspected through extracted text.
- OCR, translation, or embedding failure leaves the document visible with retry/status information.
- Evidence names the exact command, fixture, commit, or manual smoke used.

documents.title: Ticket: Maintain API foundation, auth, documents, uploads, jobs, and library

## Description

Keep apps/api as the backend owner for configuration, routing, OpenAPI, auth, documents, TUS uploads, search, settings, tags, collections, users/direct grants, ingestion file reports, and job lease/complete/fail routes.

## Acceptance

- Route or response changes update apps/api/openapi.json and generated SDK when the frontend contract changes.
- Protected API responses expose stable IDs and never filesystem paths.
- Relevant unit, integration, and contract tests are added or updated for behavior changes.
- make test-fast or a narrower justified command passes and is recorded as evidence.

documents.title: Ticket: Resolve processing status vocabulary and job timelines
documents.content:
# Ticket: Resolve processing status vocabulary and job timelines

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215134-e395 |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-214940-f930 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:51:34+02:00 |

## Description

Align the documented processing states with API, SDK, worker, and UI behavior so agents and users can rely on one status model and useful processing history.

## Acceptance

- Document status values are intentionally chosen and reflected consistently in OpenAPI, SDK types, API responses, worker updates, and UI labels.
- Job complete/fail updates preserve enough step/event history for document detail and troubleshooting.
- Any migration or compatibility concern is documented before changing persisted values.
- Contract tests cover the chosen status values and event visibility.

documents.title: Ticket: Maintain watcher registration and document lifecycle
documents.content:
# Ticket: Maintain watcher registration and document lifecycle

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215214-418c |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-214948-2ed0 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:52:14+02:00 |

## Description

Keep the worker reconciler and orchestrator responsible for scanning the configured document directory, waiting for stable writes, hashing files, registering stable files with the API, handling duplicates/changed paths/deletes, and enqueueing index jobs while TUS remains only an upload path.

## Acceptance

- Partial writes do not enqueue jobs until size and mtime are stable.
- Duplicate checksums, same-path changes, deleted files, unreadable files, and symlink/path safety are tested or intentionally documented.
- Generated artifacts are written under app data and never inside the document directory.
- Worker-to-API registration routes remain protected by worker/admin auth.

documents.title: Ticket: Complete extraction OCR translation embedding pipeline
documents.content:
# Ticket: Complete extraction OCR translation embedding pipeline

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215259-801c |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-214948-2ed0 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:52:59+02:00 |

## Description

Keep the worker processing pipeline able to extract text from supported formats, OCR scanned/text-poor PDFs with configured languages, chunk by page then length, detect language, translate chunks outside DRIVE_MAIN_LANGUAGE, embed original and translated chunks, and return records for full-text and vector indexing.

## Acceptance

- TXT, RTF, DOCX, ODT, native PDF, and scanned/text-poor PDF behavior is covered by focused or heavy tests as appropriate.
- Translation or embedding provider failure records partial step status and does not hide the document or block original-language search.
- Artifacts include enough tool/version data to support future reprocessing decisions.
- Heavy proof covers real OCR fixtures, pgvector ranking, DOCX, and scanned PDF smoke before this is marked complete.

## Description

Maintain the web console in apps/web as a quiet operational UI for household document management, using Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, OXC, lucide icons, shadcn-style components, and generated SDK-backed API helpers.

## Acceptance

- The UI supports setup/login, document list, upload, search, detail with preview/text/events, settings, tags, collections, retry, and sign out.
- Frontend API calls use packages/sdk generated functions or a thin local wrapper over them, not independent endpoint contracts.
- Typecheck and OXC lint pass for frontend changes.
- Responsive layout avoids overlapping text and keeps document workflows efficient.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215356-8508 |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-215019-f0f1 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:53:56+02:00 |

## Description

Keep access control aligned with docs/access-control-plan.md: first-run setup creates the initial admin, admins manage users and direct document grants, users see only granted documents, and ACLs are enforced at the API layer without filesystem permission tricks.

## Acceptance

- No-user startup requires setup before normal login.
- Protected routes reject unauthenticated requests and non-admins cannot manage users/settings/grants.
- Direct document grants allow and revoke user access as expected.
- Path traversal and direct-path guessing are rejected without leaking filesystem paths.

documents.title: Ticket: Smoke Docker Compose backend deployment
documents.content:
# Ticket: Smoke Docker Compose backend deployment

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215416-2aa6 |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-215009-bfda |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:54:16+02:00 |

## Description

Keep deploy/docker-compose.yml, deploy/example.env, Dockerfiles, and Make targets aligned with the self-hosted backend stack: Postgres pgvector, one-shot migrate, API, and always-on worker with document/app-data mounts.

## Acceptance

- make docker-smoke succeeds or any failure is captured with exact logs and follow-up work.
- Compose startup order remains postgres healthy -> migrate complete -> api healthy -> worker.
- Required env covers documents, app data, DB data, worker token, main language, OCR languages, and Postgres credentials.
- Worker image includes Poppler and Tesseract language packages required by default OCR settings.

documents.title: Ticket: Follow repo gates and Maat operating loop
documents.content:
# Ticket: Follow repo gates and Maat operating loop

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215436-c715 |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-215034-ba07 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:54:36+02:00 |

## Description

Use the repo and Maat processes consistently so agents can coordinate: claim/create tickets before material work, include descriptions and acceptance criteria, comment meaningful progress, complete only with evidence, and run the right validation before handoff.

## Acceptance

- Before material work, Maat status and project state are inspected and a relevant ticket is claimed or created.
- New Maat goals include outcomes and new tickets include descriptions plus acceptance criteria.
- Code changes obey ownership boundaries, update OpenAPI/SDK when contracts change, and run make check-code-lines before commit.
- Completion evidence names tests, commits, PRs, or exact manual verification.

documents.title: Ticket: Rebuild Driver Maat memory with structured fields

# Ticket: Rebuild Driver Maat memory with structured fields

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215500-697b |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-215034-ba07 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:55:00+02:00 |

## Description

Recreate the Driver Maat goals, tickets, and status events after old Driver entries were deleted, using the current schema requirements for goal outcomes, ticket descriptions, ticket acceptance criteria, and searchable data comments.

## Acceptance

- Driver has active goals with non-empty Outcome sections for product, backend, worker, frontend, deployment/configuration, access control, and repo process.
- Driver has tickets with non-empty Description sections and acceptance bullets for the major workstreams from the repo docs.
- Each workstream ticket has a comment with source docs, current repo paths, implementation status, and verification data where available.
- maat validate passes after the rebuild.

documents.title: Ticket: Document goal and ticket agent orchestration
documents.content:
# Ticket: Document goal and ticket agent orchestration

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-110449-b8b5 |
| Project | driver |
| Goal | G-20260526-215034-ba07 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T11:04:49+02:00 |

## Description

Add the requested operating model to persistent repo and Maat instructions: one goal-level agent judges and coordinates each goal, ticket-level agents do implementation work, completed ticket work returns to the goal agent for acceptance verification, and agents are closed after committed work.

## Acceptance

- AGENTS.md documents the goal-agent and ticket-agent responsibilities.
- Maat has an event/comment recording the orchestration rule and verification flow.
- The workflow states that ticket agents must commit their work before goal-agent verification and shutdown.
- No product code behavior changes are made.

documents.title: Catalog Pattern: Background Refresh
documents.content:
# Catalog Pattern: Background Refresh

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern ID | CP-20260527-background-refresh |
| Project | maat |
| Slug | background-refresh |
| Title | Background refresh |
| Category | background refresh |
| Tags | #tui #refresh #state |

## Problem

Long-running terminal sessions can become misleading when the screen does not reveal stale or refreshed state.

## Observed In

- btop
- gh-dash

## Maat Relevance

Maat should make reloads, storage freshness, and refresh failures clear while preserving navigation context.

## Implementation Notes

Keep refresh state visible, recoverable, and safe for concurrent Markdown updates.

## Related Goals

- G-20260526-220308-c0d2

## Related Tickets

- T-20260526-220336-a45d

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern ID | CP-20260527-empty-states |
| Project | maat |
| Slug | empty-states |
| Title | Empty states |
| Category | error and empty states |
| Tags | #tui #empty-states #readability |

## Problem

Empty panes and filtered-out lists can look broken unless they explain the current state and next useful action.

## Observed In

- lazygit
- superfile

## Maat Relevance

Maat should make empty projects, no matching tickets, and missing catalog entries understandable without relying only on color.

## Implementation Notes

Use concise text, stable panel dimensions, and no-color indicators for selected or empty states.

## Related Goals

- G-20260527-104618-2535

## Related Tickets

- T-20260527-104802-f29d

documents.title: Catalog Pattern: Keyboard Model
documents.content:
# Catalog Pattern: Keyboard Model

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern ID | CP-20260527-keyboard-model |
| Project | maat |
| Slug | keyboard-model |
| Title | Keyboard model |
| Category | keyboard model |
| Tags | #tui #navigation #keyboard-first |

## Problem

Terminal apps become slow when movement, inspection, filtering, and escape paths are inconsistent.

## Observed In

- lazygit
- btop
- gh-dash
- superfile

## Maat Relevance

Maat should use predictable keys for moving through projects, opening boards, inspecting tickets, filtering, refreshing, and backing out.

## Implementation Notes

Favor arrow keys and vim-style movement, enter for inspect, slash for search, and backspace or escape for return paths.

## Related Goals

- G-20260527-104618-2535

## Related Tickets

- T-20260527-104802-f29d

# Event: ticket.commented

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Event ID | E-20260529-142406-casprine-2757 |
| Time | 2026-05-29T14:24:06+02:00 |
| Actor | casprine |
| Project | maat |
| Type | ticket.commented |
| Object | T-20260529-141749-4385 |

## Summary

Reviewer synthesis: core change is storage resolution, not project inference. loadStoreAndRest must stop falling through to cwd when config is missing, empty, malformed, or unreadable. Keep cwd-based linked-project inference only after storage is already resolved. Acceptance should cover goal create, ticket create, search, and index rebuild without configured storage failing before dot-maat is created; configured storage from arbitrary cwd; and explicit storage plus project inference.

documents.title: Event: ticket.commented

# Event: ticket.commented

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Event ID | E-20260529-142406-casprine-ca64 |

| Actor | casprine |
| Project | maat |
| Type | ticket.commented |
| Object | T-20260529-141759-1cd9 |

## Summary

Reviewer synthesis: setup ergonomics also includes setup prompt defaults and setup rules, not only initialize and project link. Missing config from a Git source repo must not create projects, setup.md, or dot-maat in cwd. Decide whether setup may default to cwd at all; if it can, require explicit confirmation that the current directory is intended storage.

documents.title: Event: ticket.commented
documents.content:
# Event: ticket.commented

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Event ID | E-20260529-142407-casprine-27fc |
| Time | 2026-05-29T14:24:07+02:00 |
| Actor | casprine |
| Project | maat |
| Type | ticket.commented |
| Object | T-20260529-141807-e579 |

## Summary

Reviewer synthesis: one representative command is under-scoped. Cover every index-writing route or at least the highest-risk paths: search with missing config, search with configured storage from source cwd, index rebuild with missing config, sync without status, project link, initialize, and one write mutation. Include human, JSON, and agent-use error paths where practical, and assert both source cwd has no dot-maat and storage dot-maat exists when indexing is expected.

documents.title: Event: ticket.commented
documents.content:
# Event: ticket.commented

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Event ID | E-20260529-142422-casprine-ebf5 |
| Time | 2026-05-29T14:24:22+02:00 |
| Actor | casprine |
| Project | maat |
| Type | ticket.commented |
| Object | T-20260529-141820-ecec |

## Summary

Reviewer synthesis: docs need to say dot-maat belongs under configured storage, not product repos during normal use. Update storage-model wording, macOS app architecture command examples that omit explicit storage, README/install/search-index cache descriptions to say cache files are not committed, setup docs to state bare commands use configured storage after setup, and architecture wording that overstates SQLite as general fast reads.

documents.title: Event: ticket.commented
documents.content:
# Event: ticket.commented

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Event ID | E-20260529-142423-casprine-5c59 |
| Time | 2026-05-29T14:24:23+02:00 |
| Actor | casprine |
| Project | maat |
| Type | ticket.commented |
| Object | T-20260529-141814-2980 |

## Summary

Reviewer synthesis: lower-level index guards cannot be the policy boundary because WriteIndex and RebuildSQLiteIndex only receive paths and cannot know whether a path came from cwd fallback. Make command-level resolved storage the boundary. Lower-level APIs can still reject empty or non-absolute stores as invariant checks, and the ticket should explicitly decide whether custom SQLite Path is test-only or supported public behavior.

## Summary

Reviewer synthesis: this ticket exists because Maat sync pathspec exclusion is not the same guarantee as storage repo ignore rules. Decide explicitly whether dot-maat should remain visible to plain Git status or be ignored by setup and doctor. Tie the decision back to G-20260529-141731-daf6 because cache placement depends on strict storage resolution.

## Description

Keep the generated initialize instructions focused on the daily agent loop and required command forms instead of long background documentation.

## Acceptance

- The generated snippet includes setup, read, create or claim, comment, complete, validate, and sync commands.
- The snippet avoids obsolete flags and fits comfortably in an agent instruction file.

documents.title: Ticket: Clarify auto-pull permission warnings

# Ticket: Clarify auto-pull permission warnings

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215622-1c53 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260526-215622-fb74 |
| Status | done |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:56:22+02:00 |

## Description

Make auto-pull failures clear when storage exists but the agent cannot perform network or Git operations, without masking the command result.

## Acceptance

- Human output explains that the Markdown read or write still happened when only auto-pull failed.
- Agent-use output emits a warning update with the Git error and storage path.

documents.title: Ticket: Show storage access hint on permission failures
documents.content:
# Ticket: Show storage access hint on permission failures

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260526-215622-3bd0 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260526-215622-fb74 |
| Status | done |
| Created | 2026-05-26T21:56:22+02:00 |

## Description

Improve setup, initialize, and read-path failures so permission errors explain which storage path failed, why access was denied, and what the agent should try next.

## Acceptance

- A denied read or write prints the failing path and a concrete remediation such as choosing a writable storage repo or granting access.
- JSON and agent-use output include enough structured warning detail for an agent to decide whether to retry, ask for approval, or switch storage paths.

- RunTUIWithOptions uses the pull-aware dashboard loader before first render when AutoPullBeforeRefresh is true.
- Pull warnings on initial load are surfaced without hiding successfully loaded dashboard data.
- Tests cover loader selection for initial load and refresh load.

Document the Markdown object shapes for catalog apps, reusable patterns, decisions, opportunities, and catalog review events under projects/maat/catalog. Include required fields, stable IDs, links between patterns and Maat work, and plain-Markdown examples.

## Description

Add Go types and loading/validation logic for catalog app, pattern, decision, and opportunity Markdown files. Validation should catch missing IDs, malformed links, duplicate slugs, invalid decision states, and broken references to goals or tickets when those links are present.

## Acceptance

- Catalog loader returns typed objects from Markdown files under projects/<project-key>/catalog.
- Validation reports missing required fields, duplicate object IDs or slugs, invalid states, and broken object links.
- Unit tests cover valid fixtures and at least three malformed catalog objects.

Create initial catalog fixtures or sample objects from the IA: lazygit, btop, gh-dash, and superfile as apps; focused detail pane, keyboard model, background refresh, and empty states as patterns. Keep metadata honest when unknown by marking it unknown instead of inventing values.

- Initial catalog objects exist as plain Markdown and validate successfully.
- Each seeded app has name, slug, summary, source link, category, tags, and last reviewed date.
- Each seeded pattern explains the problem solved, observed apps, Maat relevance, and at least one related goal or ticket link where known.

Add maat catalog list and maat catalog show commands for humans. List should support apps, patterns, decisions, and opportunities with compact scannable columns. Show should render a structured detail view for one object.

## Description

Refine the primary TUI path so a human can see all projects, open one into a Kanban-style board, move through board items, and read a focused detail view with title, description, acceptance criteria, status, ownership, related goal, and recent events.

## Acceptance

- Project list remains the first TUI screen for project-management use.
- Opening a project leads to a board-like ticket view, and opening a ticket leads to a readable detail view.
- The ticket detail includes description, acceptance criteria, current status, owner or claim information, related goal, and recent comments or completion evidence when available.

Create the initial Tauri macOS app structure for Maat with a native window, app lifecycle hooks, settings storage, and a minimal command-runner bridge capable of invoking a configured maat binary.

documents.title: Ticket: Build the first-run storage setup assistant

# Ticket: Build the first-run storage setup assistant

| Field | Value |
