| Goal | G-20260527-122622-7317 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:27:10+02:00 |

## Description

Create the desktop setup flow for selecting, creating, or cloning a Git-backed Maat storage repository, then configure the CLI with maat setup and validate it with setup doctor.

## Acceptance

- A user can create a new local storage repo, select an existing repo, or enter a clone URL when clone support is enabled.
- The app runs maat setup --storage <path> --actor <name> --json after storage selection.
- The app runs maat setup doctor --storage <path> --fix --json and shows blocking checks, warnings, and safe fixes.

Package a signed maat binary inside the macOS app and install or update an app-private copy under Application Support during first-run setup.

## Acceptance

- The app bundle includes the correct darwin maat binary for the target architecture.
- On first launch, the bundled binary can install or refresh ~/Library/Application Support/maat/bin/maat using maat update --source ... --json.
- The app records the installed CLI path and verifies it with maat version --json before using it.

documents.title: Ticket: Keep first-run sync choices conservative

| Field | Value |

| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260527-122622-7317 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:27:10+02:00 |

## Description

Design setup defaults so the app does not add remotes, push commits, or enable auto-push unless the user explicitly chooses those behaviors.

## Acceptance

- Auto-push is not enabled by default in the desktop setup flow.
- Adding a storage remote or pushing storage state requires a clear user action.
- The setup flow communicates that Markdown in the storage Git repo remains the source of truth.

Change storage resolution so normal commands use explicit --storage or configured storage and otherwise fail instead of treating the current working directory as storage.

- Existing configured-storage use still works without passing --storage.

documents.title: Ticket: Add regression tests for source repo cache creation

| Field | Value |

## Description

Cover the exact regression where search, index rebuild, doctor fix, initialize, or write flows create .maat in the product repo because storage fell back to cwd.

## Acceptance

- A test runs a representative read/search command from a non-storage source repo with missing config and asserts no .maat directory is created.
- A test runs the same command with configured storage and asserts the cache lands under configured storage/.maat.
- The test suite covers both human and JSON error paths where practical.

Make index refresh code write only under the already resolved storage root and fail loudly when storage resolution is absent or ambiguous.

- WriteIndex and SQLite rebuild callers receive an explicit resolved storage path.
- Index refresh never independently falls back to cwd.
- Failures report that indexes are rebuildable and identify the intended storage path when available.

documents.title: Ticket: Document configured storage and cache boundaries
documents.content:
# Ticket: Document configured storage and cache boundaries

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260529-141820-ecec |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260529-141740-65d8 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-29T14:18:20+02:00 |

## Description

Update user and agent documentation so configured storage is the normal operating mode and cache files are described as storage-local rebuildable artifacts.

## Acceptance

- README and install/search docs explain that normal commands use configured storage after setup.
- Docs state that <storage>/.maat/index.json and <storage>/.maat/index.sqlite are rebuildable and not committed.
- Docs no longer imply source repos may safely receive .maat caches during normal use.

documents.title: Ticket: Decide and enforce storage cache ignore policy
documents.content:
# Ticket: Decide and enforce storage cache ignore policy

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260529-142348-4b56 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260529-141740-65d8 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-29T14:23:48+02:00 |

## Description

Decide whether storage-local .maat cache files are only excluded by Maat sync pathspecs or also ignored by the storage repo itself, then make tooling and docs match that decision.

## Acceptance

- The project explicitly decides whether setup should create or maintain a storage .gitignore entry for .maat/.
- If .gitignore enforcement is chosen, setup or setup doctor creates or validates the ignore entry without touching product repos.
- If pathspec-only exclusion is chosen, docs and tests state that normal Git commands can still see .maat as local untracked or modified cache files.

documents.title: Ticket: Implement Core Agent token lifecycle API
documents.content:
# Ticket: Implement Core Agent token lifecycle API

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260528-034705-69b4 |
| Project | orion |
| Goal | G-20260528-033358-9a6b |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-28T03:47:05+02:00 |

## Description

Implement the Core side of Agent token lifecycle controls from docs/architecture/agent-token-lifecycle.md: token hash/version/revocation metadata, admin-authenticated rotate revoke reissue and status actions, revoked-token auth rejection, registration safeguards for revoked machine_id recovery, one-time token responses, redaction, audit events, and preserved Agent/monitor identity.

## Acceptance

- Core migrations, services, API handlers, route annotations, OpenAPI, and tests cover rotate revoke reissue and status; old tokens are rejected after rotation; revoked Agents cannot recover a token through unauthenticated /v1/register; agent_id machine_id monitor IDs reports incidents and maintenance state are preserved; normal Agent and frontend responses do not expose plaintext tokens.

## Description

Implement the Agent-side recovery UX from docs/architecture/agent-token-lifecycle.md: apply an admin-issued replacement token to existing local state without clearing agent_id or monitor mappings, keep reconfigure for intentional identity reset, and surface Core token revoked or invalid auth failures as redacted visible terminal errors.

## Acceptance

- Agent CLI and state tests cover applying a replacement token while preserving agent_id core_url and monitor mappings; auth failures from protected routes stop reporting with a non-zero visible error and do not queue new reports with rejected credentials; docs explain when to use token apply versus reconfigure.
